title: Pupalia remotiflora
taxonomy: Pupalia remotiflora is a species of plant in the family Amaranthaceae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as amaranths. The Amaranthaceae family is further divided into several subfamilies, and Pupalia remotiflora falls under the subfamily Amaranthoideae. As a member of the Amaranthaceae family, Pupalia remotiflora is a dicotyledonous plant, meaning it has two cotyledons or seed leaves in its seedling stage. The plant is native to tropical and subtropical regions, and its classification reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants in the Amaranthaceae family. Pupalia remotiflora's classification is also used by botanists and taxonomists to understand its biology and ecology.

intro: Pupalia remotiflora, also known as the 'False Pupalia', is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the family Amaranthaceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with health benefits including the treatment of fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The bioactive constituents of Pupalia remotiflora include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. In traditional medicine, Pupalia remotiflora is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, or made into a powder for use in capsules or tablets, and is also used to make herbal teas and tinctures.
